compiled_state.title: Implement _is_eagain_failure() helper and EAGAIN detection in record-test-status.sh
compiled_state.author: Main
compiled_state.description:
## Description

Add EAGAIN resource-exhaustion detection to record-test-status.sh using an extracted helper function. When a test fails with exit code 254 AND stderr/stdout contains EAGAIN patterns, classify as resource_exhaustion instead of failed.

## Implementation

1. Add EAGAIN_PATTERN regex variable near top of script (after sourcing libs):
   EAGAIN_PATTERN='fork: (retry: )?Resource temporarily unavailable|BlockingIOError.*Resource temporarily unavailable'

2. Add _is_eagain_failure() helper function (follows existing _write_partial_status pattern):
   Takes exit_code and output_file as args. Returns 0 when exit_code==254 AND grep -qE "$EAGAIN_PATTERN" "$output_file".

3. CRITICAL: Insert EAGAIN check BEFORE rm -f "$test_output_file" in the per-file loop. The check must happen while the output file still exists. Insert at line ~966 (after exit_code is captured, before any cleanup). If _is_eagain_failure returns 0, set STATUS="resource_exhaustion" (respecting severity hierarchy) and continue.

4. Update severity hierarchy: timeout > failed > resource_exhaustion > passed. In the per-file loop severity check (line ~1104-1105), add: resource_exhaustion does not override failed or timeout. In the --source-file merge block (lines ~1188-1194), add resource_exhaustion handling between failed and passed.

5. Deference check: In the --source-file merge block, when existing status is "passed" and new STATUS is "resource_exhaustion", keep "passed" (suite-engine result is authoritative).

## Files to Modify
- plugins/dso/hooks/record-test-status.sh (EDIT)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All RED tests from Task 1 now PASS (GREEN)
  Verify: bash tests/hooks/test-record-test-status-eagain.sh
- [ ] _is_eagain_failure function exists in record-test-status.sh
  Verify: grep -q '_is_eagain_failure' plugins/dso/hooks/record-test-status.sh
- [ ] EAGAIN_PATTERN variable defined
  Verify: grep -q 'EAGAIN_PATTERN' plugins/dso/hooks/record-test-status.sh
- [ ] resource_exhaustion in severity hierarchy handling
  Verify: grep -q 'resource_exhaustion' plugins/dso/hooks/record-test-status.sh
- [ ] Deference check: existing passed not overwritten by resource_exhaustion
  Verify: bash tests/hooks/test-record-test-status-eagain.sh 2>&1 | grep -q 'test_eagain_deference_to_existing_passed.*PASS'
- [ ] Existing test suites still pass
  Verify: bash tests/hooks/test-record-test-status-part1.sh
- [ ] No syntax errors
  Verify: bash -n plugins/dso/hooks/record-test-status.sh

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 2/6: Code patterns understood ✓ — EAGAIN_PATTERN and _is_eagain_failure() studied in tests/lib/suite-engine.sh; record-test-status.sh structure analyzed (per-file loop at ~966, merge block at ~1188-1194)
comments.author: Test

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 3/6: RED tests confirmed — PASSED: 4  FAILED: 4 (test_eagain_exit254_with_stderr_pattern, test_eagain_deference_to_existing_passed, test_eagain_blocking_io_error_pattern, test_merge_path all FAIL as expected)
comments.author: Test

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 4/6: Implementation complete ✓ — Added EAGAIN_PATTERN + _is_eagain_failure() helper; inserted EAGAIN check before cleanup in per-file loop; updated severity hierarchy in merge block; fixed stale guard to skip in --source-file mode (enabling correct deference to existing passed)
comments.author: Test

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 5/6: GREEN confirmed — test-record-test-status-eagain.sh: PASSED: 8 FAILED: 0 — test-record-test-status-part1.sh: PASSED: 13 FAILED: 0 — bash -n syntax check: OK
